objective: Implement the read-only always-on observer slice from epic #224 so governance can produce deterministic per-repo health reports under project report paths without enqueuing work packets or mutating governed repos.
scope_boundary: Create a repo-local read-only observer that loads the governed repo registry, rule artifacts, graphify summaries, compendium, closeouts, backlog, and raw issue feed metadata.
Write observer output only under `projects/<repo_slug>/reports/` by default, with explicit override support for tests.
Include source commit SHAs and SHA-256 artifact hashes for registry, graph, wiki, compendium, backlog, closeouts, and issue feed inputs.
Report per-repo health, stale knowledge signals, planning-gate gaps, and daemon readiness without making LLM-only pass/fail decisions.
Add launchd SOPs, a launchd plist template, tests, registry documentation, cross-review, and closeout evidence.
out_of_scope: Installing or bootstrapping the launchd agent on the operator machine.
Enqueuing packets, dispatching workers, mutating governed repositories, or starting a daemon process.
Adding local model runtime, PII classifier services, or packet queue state machines from later #224 phases.
Reading or storing raw issue bodies; the observer may use metadata-only raw issue feed files.
Writing to the shared dirty main checkout.
research_summary: Issue #227 follows the #224 Phase 3 design: introduce read-only observation before daemon or queue authority. Existing #225 registry metadata provides project paths, graph/wiki paths, repo local paths, and enabled subsystem flags. Existing #226 planning-scope gate requires this governance-surface work to have an issue-specific structured plan and accepted review. The implementation uses a stdlib Python observer that inspects committed governance artifacts and local git state, computes content hashes, writes deterministic JSON/Markdown reports to project report directories, and leaves `raw/packets/` untouched. The launchd template is not installed by this slice; the runbook documents bootstrap, kickstart, bootout, and kill/disable commands for a later operator action.

sprints.name: Observer Core
sprints.goal: Produce deterministic read-only governance health reports from existing committed artifacts.
tasks: Add `scripts/orchestrator/read_only_observer.py` using only Python stdlib and the governed repo registry.
Load graph reports, wiki index state, compendium, closeouts, backlog, and raw issue metadata where available.
Compute artifact hashes and source commit SHAs for each repository.
Write JSON and Markdown reports under `projects/<repo_slug>/reports/`.
Ensure no packet queue writes happen in this slice.
acceptance_criteria: Observer writes reports only under approved report paths by default.
Reports include source commit SHAs and artifact hashes.
Reports include stale knowledge, planning-gate, and daemon readiness sections.

sprints.name: launchd SOP
sprints.goal: Document a disabled-by-default local launchd path for reboot-survivable read-only observation.
tasks: Add a launchd plist template with explicit repo root, report root, log paths, and interval.
Document bootstrap, kickstart, health check, bootout, and kill/disable commands.
Validate the plist with local syntax checks.
acceptance_criteria: The launchd template is syntactically valid.
The runbook documents after-reboot behavior and kill/disable SOP.
The slice does not install the plist automatically.

sprints.name: Governance Evidence
sprints.goal: Record the observer in governance registries and closeout artifacts.
tasks: Register the feature, service, and data contract.
Add PDCAR, cross-review, and closeout artifacts.
Refresh graph/wiki and compendium after closeout.
acceptance_criteria: Feature, service, and data dictionary docs describe the observer and report contract.
Alternate-family review passes or required findings are resolved.
Closeout hook completes and graph/wiki/compendium are current.

specialist_reviews.focus: Read-only file access, bounded report write paths, packet non-interference, launchd SOPs, and validation coverage.
specialist_reviews.summary: The implementation is constrained to report generation and launchd documentation. It does not dispatch packets or mutate governed repos.

alternate_model_review.summary: Alternate-family review required for Tier 1 governance automation. Findings are recorded in the #227 cross-review artifact and must be resolved or explicitly accepted before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement #227 read-only observer, launchd template/runbook, tests, report directories, documentation, review, and closeout only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Build observer and docs, run tests and validators, obtain alternate-family review, close out, open PR, and merge after CI passes.

material_deviation_rules: If observer needs issue bodies or live GitHub API calls, defer to a follow-up issue; this slice uses metadata-only local artifacts.
If launchd installation is required, stop and create a separate operator-run task; this slice only provides a template and SOP.
If a report cannot read a source artifact, record the missing artifact in the report instead of inventing a status.
If any packet queue write is needed, halt because packet enqueue authority belongs to a later #224 phase.
approved_by: operator instruction: proceed, loop through, no HITL
